rules.name: Row count exact match to Bronze (3,039 +/- 5)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 3034 OR cnt > 3044 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: Silver row count must be exactly 3,039 (matching Bronze 1:1), allowing +/- 5 row tolerance for refresh-cycle drift. The Silver transformation is 1:1 with Bronze -- no filtering, no splitting, no aggregation. Any deviation beyond +/- 5 indicates a transformation defect or a Bronze count change that should trigger investigation.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Bronze (after PREDDEG=3 OR ICLEVEL=1 filter, deduped on unitid): 3,039. Silver (1:1 with Bronze; no filtering, no splitting): 3,039. Distinct unitid in Silver: 3,039. unitid duplicates: 0.' Threshold: exact count 3,039 with +/- 5 tolerance for annual data refreshes.

rules.name: record_id uniqueness
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.college_scorecard_institution GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: record_id is the Silver surrogate primary key, computed deterministically from unitid via compute_grain_id() with prefix 'csi'. Must be unique across all 3,039 rows. Any duplicate indicates a Silver transformation defect.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'record_id unique, non-null: derived from unitid, 100%.' Since record_id is a deterministic hash of unitid, and unitid is verified unique (0 duplicates across 3,039 rows), record_id uniqueness is guaranteed by construction. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness and non-null
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN null_cnt > 0 OR dup_cnt > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN unitid IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS null_cnt, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT unitid FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE unitid IS NOT NULL GROUP BY unitid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1)) AS dup_cnt FROM base.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: unitid is the natural grain key for the Silver table (one row per institution). Must be unique and non-null across all rows. IPEDS identifiers are always assigned as positive integers; absence or duplication breaks the institution grain and downstream join integrity to base.college_scorecard and consumable.career_outcomes.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Distinct unitid in Silver: 3,039. unitid duplicates: 0.' Bronze EDA confirms UNITID is 100% populated. Silver is 1:1 with Bronze, so uniqueness and non-null are both guaranteed at load. Threshold: 0 violations (strict).

rules.name: record_id non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE record_id IS NULL
rules.description: record_id is the Silver primary key and must be present in every row. Since it is computed deterministically from unitid (which is verified non-null), a null record_id would indicate a transformation bug in compute_grain_id().
rules.evidence: EDA: 'record_id unique, non-null.' Derived by compute_grain_id(row, ['unitid'], prefix='csi') -- null unitid would produce null record_id, and unitid is verified 100% non-null. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: institution_control valid enum values
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE institution_control NOT IN ('Public', 'Private nonprofit', 'Private for-profit')
rules.description: institution_control must be exactly one of the three canonical labels: 'Public', 'Private nonprofit', or 'Private for-profit'. This derived field is produced from raw control {1, 2, 3} -- any other value indicates a mapping defect. This field routes downstream public/private logic.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'institution_control label is 100% populated with zero unmapped values. Raw control only contains {1, 2, 3} (867 / 1,754 / 418). No null control cases, no out-of-domain values.' Distribution: Public 867 (28.53%), Private nonprofit 1,754 (57.72%), Private for-profit 418 (13.75%), unmapped 0 (0.00%). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: institution_control 100% non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE institution_control IS NULL
rules.description: institution_control is a NOT NULL derived field required by the logical model. Since raw control is 100% populated and mapped via a deterministic CASE, nulls here indicate a transformation defect.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'institution_control label is 100% populated with zero unmapped values.' Domain constraint from logical model: 'institution_control -- NOT NULL.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: net_price_annual <= cost_of_attendance_annual (both non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE net_price_annual IS NOT NULL AND cost_of_attendance_annual IS NOT NULL AND net_price_annual > cost_of_attendance_annual
rules.description: Net price (what a student pays after aid) must never exceed cost of attendance (sticker price) for any institution. If net_price > COA, aid would be negative -- impossible by construction. EDA confirmed 0 violations across 2,233 rows where both fields are non-null.
rules.evidence: EDA Invariant 1: 'Rows with both non-null: 2,233. Violations: 0. Rows with NP == COA (zero aid): 4. Max COA - NP (largest aid package): $70,026. Median COA - NP: $11,081.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: net_price_4yr = net_price_annual * 4 (IEEE-754 exact)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE net_price_annual IS NOT NULL AND (net_price_4yr IS NULL OR ABS(net_price_4yr - (net_price_annual * 4)) > 0.01)
rules.description: net_price_4yr is derived as net_price_annual * 4 and must exactly equal that product within floating-point epsilon ($0.01 tolerance for safety). The derivation is null-preserving so a non-null net_price_annual must produce a non-null net_price_4yr. Multiplying a finite DOUBLE by 4 is lossless in IEEE-754.
rules.evidence: EDA Invariant 2: 'Rows with non-null net_price_annual: 2,233. Violations (|derived - annual*4| > $1): 0.' Additional finding: 'The 4yr derivation is IEEE-754 exact: zero tolerance violations for net_price_4yr = net_price_annual * 4 (0 of 2,233 non-null rows). This is expected -- multiplying a finite double by 4 is lossless.' Threshold: 0 violations with $0.01 tolerance.

rules.name: cost_of_attendance_4yr = cost_of_attendance_annual * 4 (IEEE-754 exact)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE cost_of_attendance_annual IS NOT NULL AND (cost_of_attendance_4yr IS NULL OR ABS(cost_of_attendance_4yr - (cost_of_attendance_annual * 4)) > 0.01)
rules.description: cost_of_attendance_4yr is derived as cost_of_attendance_annual * 4 and must exactly equal that product within floating-point epsilon ($0.01 tolerance). Null-preserving: non-null annual must produce non-null 4yr. Multiplying a finite DOUBLE by 4 is lossless.
rules.evidence: EDA Invariant 3: 'Rows with non-null cost_of_attendance_annual: 2,233. Violations: 0.' Same IEEE-754 exactness guarantee as the net_price_4yr invariant. Threshold: 0 violations with $0.01 tolerance.

rules.name: net_price_annual non-null coverage >= 70% overall
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(np_present AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) < 0.70 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN net_price_annual IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS np_present FROM base.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: At least 70% of rows must have net_price_annual populated. The logical model's draft 85% threshold will fail hard -- the EDA shows actual coverage is 73.48%. The 70% floor provides ~3.5pp margin above observed coverage to tolerate annual data refresh drift while still catching coverage degradation.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Silver net_price_annual non-null is 73.48%, not the 85% assumed in the logical model's range constraints. The logical model's >=85% non-null P0 threshold for net_price_annual will fail hard.' Observed: 2,233 / 3,039 = 73.48%. Recommendation: 'Use >= 70% for both cost_of_attendance_annual and net_price_annual at P0.' Threshold: 70%.

rules.name: cost_of_attendance_annual non-null coverage >= 70% overall
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(coa_present AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) < 0.70 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN cost_of_attendance_annual IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS coa_present FROM base.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: At least 70% of rows must have cost_of_attendance_annual populated. EDA confirms this field and net_price_annual are row-identical in nullness (Key Finding #1) -- the same 2,233 rows populate both. Spec's 90% and logical model's 80% thresholds would fail hard. 70% provides ~3.5pp margin above observed 73.48%.
rules.evidence: EDA Key Finding #1: 'Coverage of net_price_annual and cost_of_attendance_annual is identical down to the row. 2,233 rows have both populated, 806 have both null, and zero rows have one without the other.' EDA: 'cost_of_attendance_annual non-null coverage: logical model >=80%, actual 73.48%, recommended >=70%.' Threshold: 70%.

rules.name: Public institution (control=1) net_price_annual coverage >= 85%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(np_present AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total_public AS DOUBLE)) < 0.85 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN institution_control = 'Public' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS total_public, SUM(CASE WHEN institution_control = 'Public' AND net_price_annual IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS np_present FROM base.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: Public institutions (control=1) must have net_price_annual populated for at least 85% of rows. Public schools have the strongest reporting discipline among the three control types. Observed coverage is 89.27% -- the 85% floor provides ~4pp margin for refresh drift.
rules.evidence: EDA Coverage by Institution Control: 'Public: N=867, Both populated=774, 89.27%.' Recommendation: 'net_price_annual coverage, Public: >= 85%, Safe.' Control-stratified coverage allows asymmetric thresholds reflecting the actual reporting behavior of each control type. Threshold: 85% of Public institutions.

rules.name: Private nonprofit (control=2) net_price_annual coverage >= 65%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(np_present AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total_priv_np AS DOUBLE)) < 0.65 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN institution_control = 'Private nonprofit' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS total_priv_np, SUM(CASE WHEN institution_control = 'Private nonprofit' AND net_price_annual IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS np_present FROM base.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: Private nonprofit institutions (control=2) must have net_price_annual populated for at least 65% of rows. Observed coverage is 70.64% -- the 65% floor provides ~5.5pp margin. Lower than Public because private nonprofits report NPT4_PRIV with known gaps for smaller institutions.
rules.evidence: EDA Coverage by Institution Control: 'Private nonprofit: N=1,754, Both populated=1,239, 70.64%.' Recommendation: 'net_price_annual coverage, Private nonprofit: >= 65%, Lower threshold.' Domain context: 'Private nonprofit coverage below spec. Lower to >= 65%.' Threshold: 65% of Private nonprofit institutions.

rules.name: Private for-profit (control=3) net_price_annual coverage >= 50%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(np_present AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total_priv_fp AS DOUBLE)) < 0.50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN institution_control = 'Private for-profit' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS total_priv_fp, SUM(CASE WHEN institution_control = 'Private for-profit' AND net_price_annual IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS np_present FROM base.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: Private for-profit institutions (control=3) must have net_price_annual populated for at least 50% of rows. Observed coverage is 52.63% -- the weakest reporting segment. P2 priority because for-profit underreporting is a known systemic pattern, not a DQ-actionable defect. The 50% floor catches catastrophic coverage drops while accepting the known ceiling.
rules.evidence: EDA Coverage by Institution Control: 'Private for-profit: N=418, Both populated=220, 52.63%.' EDA Surprise #3: 'Private for-profit coverage of 52.63% is well below the spec-implied 80%.' Recommendation: 'net_price_annual coverage, Private for-profit: >= 50%, For-profit underreports.' Threshold: 50% of Private for-profit institutions (P2 informational).

rules.name: Quintile span monotonicity (net_price_q1 <= net_price_q5)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE net_price_q1 IS NOT NULL AND net_price_q5 IS NOT NULL AND net_price_q1 > net_price_q5
rules.description: Where both net_price_q1 (income $0-$30K) and net_price_q5 (income $110K+) are populated, q1 should be less than or equal to q5 -- lower-income families generally receive more aid. 46 legitimate violations exist in the current dataset (2.51% of 1,832 rows with both populated) due to federal aid caps at highest-need levels and merit-aid concentration at middle quintiles. The 50-row tolerance provides modest headroom above observed 46.
rules.evidence: EDA Invariant 4: 'Public: 7/716 (0.98%). Private nonprofit: 33/998 (3.31%). Private for-profit: 6/118 (5.08%). Overall: 46/1,832 (2.51%).' Violation magnitudes: min $164, median $1,396, max $18,121. Recommendation: 'P1 rule with >= 97% pass rate (allow ~3% violations) or <= 50 rows violating.' Adjacent-pair monotonicity (q1<=q2) explicitly rejected per EDA: 'Q1 > Q2 inversions: 37.9% for private. Do NOT use adjacent-pair monotonicity rules.' Threshold: <= 50 violations.

rules.name: net_price_annual range [-$5,000, $80,000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE net_price_annual IS NOT NULL AND (net_price_annual < -5000 OR net_price_annual > 80000)
rules.description: When non-null, net_price_annual must be between -$5,000 and $80,000. Negative values are legitimate: community colleges where average grant aid exceeds average COA (min observed -$1,180 at Skyline College). Upper bound $80,000 provides ~4% headroom above observed max $77,180. Logical model draft floor of $0 would reject legitimate data.
rules.evidence: EDA Derived Field Profile: 'net_price_annual: Min=-$1,180, Max=$77,180, Median=$18,990.' EDA Edge Case: 'Skyline College -$4,720 (4yr), San Diego Mesa -$3,616 (4yr), St Petersburg -$208 (4yr) -- community colleges where average grant aid exceeds average COA. Legitimate data. Do not set a > 0 floor.' Recommendation: 'net_price_annual in [-5000, 80000]. Negative floor required.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: cost_of_attendance_annual range [$5,000, $100,000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE cost_of_attendance_annual IS NOT NULL AND (cost_of_attendance_annual < 5000 OR cost_of_attendance_annual > 100000)
rules.description: When non-null, cost_of_attendance_annual must be between $5,000 and $100,000. Sticker price cannot legitimately be negative and cannot plausibly exceed $100K per year even for the most expensive institutions. Observed range [$6,362, $87,804] sits well within bounds, providing ~14% upper headroom.
rules.evidence: EDA Derived Field Profile: 'cost_of_attendance_annual: Min=$6,362, Max=$87,804, Median=$30,354.' EDA Recommendation: 'cost_of_attendance_annual in [5000, 100000]. Has headroom.' Consistent with Bronze rule RAW-CSI-005 for costt4_a. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_abbr matches ^[A-Z]{2}$
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE state_abbr IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(state_abbr, '^[A-Z]{2}$')
rules.description: state_abbr must match the USPS 2-letter uppercase format (^[A-Z]{2}$). This field is NOT NULL per the logical and physical models, and carries the physical CHECK (state_abbr ~ '^[A-Z]{2}$'). The 58 distinct values observed in EDA span all 50 states plus DC and US territories -- all compliant with the regex.
rules.evidence: EDA Validity Row for state_abbr: 'state_abbr matches ^[A-Z]{2}$, regex, 0 violations, 58 distinct (50 states + DC + territories), 100%, P0, Will pass.' Physical model constraint: 'CHECK (state_abbr ~ '^[A-Z]{2}$').' Domain constraint from logical model: 'state_abbr -- NOT NULL, 2-letter USPS.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: tuition_in_state range [$0, $70,000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE tuition_in_state IS NOT NULL AND (tuition_in_state < 0 OR tuition_in_state > 70000)
rules.description: When non-null, tuition_in_state must be between $0 and $70,000. The physical model CHECK was originally [$0, $65,000]; post-governance review raised the cap to $70,000 to reflect the Bronze observed max of $69,330 with modest (~1%) headroom. The physical model has been updated to match this range.
rules.evidence: EDA Validity Row: 'tuition_in_state, range, Bronze: [$600, $69,330], [$0, $70,000], P1, Raise cap to $70K.' Physical model updated from BETWEEN 0 AND 65000 to BETWEEN 0 AND 70000 per post-governance review. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: tuition_out_of_state range [$0, $75,000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE tuition_out_of_state IS NOT NULL AND (tuition_out_of_state < 0 OR tuition_out_of_state > 75000)
rules.description: When non-null, tuition_out_of_state must be between $0 and $75,000. Out-of-state tuition at public institutions and published rates at private institutions both land in this field. Observed Bronze max is $69,330; the $75,000 cap provides ~8% headroom for refresh drift. The physical model CHECK has been tightened from [$0, $80,000] to [$0, $75,000] to match.
rules.evidence: EDA Validity Row: 'tuition_out_of_state, range, Bronze: [$600, $69,330], [$0, $70,000], P1, Raise cap to $70K.' Post-governance review set the cap at $75,000 for additional headroom beyond the EDA-recommended $70,000. Physical model updated from BETWEEN 0 AND 80000 to BETWEEN 0 AND 75000 per post-governance review. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: room_board_on_campus range [$1,000, $30,000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE room_board_on_campus IS NOT NULL AND (room_board_on_campus < 1000 OR room_board_on_campus > 30000)
rules.description: When non-null, room_board_on_campus must be between $1,000 and $30,000. The original physical model CHECK of [$3,000, $25,000] was incorrect: EDA confirmed Bronze observed min is $1,000 (legitimate low-cost institutions) and max is $29,874. The physical model has been updated from [3000, 25000] to [1000, 30000] to match this rule.
rules.evidence: EDA Validity Row: 'room_board_on_campus, range, Bronze: [$1,000, $29,874], [$1,000, $30,000], P1, Widen both ends.' EDA Recommendation: 'Widen room_board_on_campus to [$1,000, $30,000].' Physical model updated from BETWEEN 3000 AND 25000 to BETWEEN 1000 AND 30000 per post-governance review to match observed evidence. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: room_board_off_campus range [$1,000, $40,000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE room_board_off_campus IS NOT NULL AND (room_board_off_campus < 1000 OR room_board_off_campus > 40000)
rules.description: When non-null, room_board_off_campus must be between $1,000 and $40,000. Bronze observed max is $39,100 at high-cost-metro institutions (including California schools). Physical model CHECK updated to BETWEEN 1000 AND 40000 to match.
rules.evidence: EDA Validity Row: 'room_board_off_campus (no draft rule), Bronze: [$2,001, $39,100], [$2,000, $40,000], P1, New rule.' Range widened to [$1,000, $40,000] per DQ re-run (initial $30K cap rejected 4 legitimate high-cost-metro institutions; Bronze observed max $39,100). Physical model updated to BETWEEN 1000 AND 40000 to match. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: books_supplies range [$0, $10,000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard_institution WHERE books_supplies IS NOT NULL AND (books_supplies < 0 OR books_supplies > 10000)
rules.description: When non-null, books_supplies must be between $0 and $10,000. Bronze observed max is $9,741 at The Citadel (uniform/materials-driven cost profile). Physical model CHECK updated to BETWEEN 0 AND 10000 to match. P2 priority: informational monitoring; books_supplies is a soft estimate rather than a transactional amount.
rules.evidence: EDA Validity Row: 'books_supplies (no draft rule), Bronze: [$0, $9,741], [$0, $10,000], P1, Allow zero.' Range widened from initial $5K cap to $10K per DQ re-run (rejected 3 legitimate institutions: The Citadel, Spartan Aeronautics, Modern College of Design). Threshold: 0 violations.
